title: Projects
text: My academic journey has encouraged my to explore and benefit from a variety of team projects. Through collaboration, I’ve been able to strengthen my communication, leadership, project management, and technical skills. I’ve found the following projects to be particularly educational and contructive forms of hands-on coursework application.

title: Projects
section: Data Science & Business Analytics Job Market Trends in 2024
text:
Data Science & Business Analytics Job Market Trends in 2024
Data-driven analysis of business analytics, data science, and machine learning trends found in 2024 job postings
Upon my intriduction to big data and cloud analytics for business, I explored job market trends amongst industries alongside a group of my classmates.

title: Projects
text:
Engineering Senior Design Project
Decarbonization of the UCONN Campus by 2030
During the senior year of my undergraduate degree, I formalized a plan to help the campus reach carbon neutrality with two of my classmates. At the time, UConn was emiting approximately 108,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide per year.
Our design project was chosen because of our shared interest in sustainability and how clean energy could be introduced in a cost effective way. Our solution combined the use of renewable energy sources and conservation policies. We created an energy balance and a mass balance to outline a net-zero carbon emmision future.

title: About
text:
Straumann Group
Quality Assurance Biotech Co-op
After aquiring my Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering…
I entered with a strong foundation in fluid mechanics, process kinetics, thermodynamics, and air pollution control…
My goal is to use these degrees as a bridge between business development and the engineering mindset to pursue my interest in process improvement and optimization.

section: Microbrewery Business Plan in South End, Boston
text:
Microbrewery Business Plan in South End, Boston
The development of a managerial report to propose a microbrewery business plan for an existing restaurant in the Boston area
For a quantitative and qualitative decision making class, we formed groups to apply our business structure coursework to a real-life scenario.
